title: Human Voice Plugin Configuration
description: Unified configuration for the human-voice plugin, covering AI writing pattern detection settings and voice elicitation interview settings. Stored at $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/config.json (or ~/.human-voice/config.json in standalone mode).

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference URI for editor validation and autocompletion.

detection.description: AI writing pattern detection settings. Controls which file types are scanned, which patterns are flagged, and how results are reported.

extensions.description: File extensions to include when scanning for AI writing patterns. Each entry should include the leading dot.

content_directories.description: Directories to scan for content files. When empty, the plugin scans the current working directory recursively.

ignore.description: Glob patterns for files and directories to exclude from scanning (e.g., 'node_modules/**', 'vendor/**').

character_patterns.description: Settings for detecting non-ASCII character substitutions commonly introduced by AI writing tools (smart punctuation, emoji injection, etc.).

enabled.description: Master toggle for all character-level pattern detection. When false, all character pattern checks are skipped.

em_dash.description: Detect em dash characters (U+2014) that AI models frequently substitute for hyphens or double-hyphens.

en_dash.description: Detect en dash characters (U+2013) inserted by AI in place of plain hyphens in ranges and compounds.

smart_quotes.description: Detect curly/smart quote characters (U+2018, U+2019, U+201C, U+201D) substituted for straight ASCII quotes.

ellipsis.description: Detect the Unicode horizontal ellipsis character (U+2026) used in place of three consecutive periods.

emojis.description: Detect emoji characters inserted by AI models, which are uncommon in most human-authored prose and documentation.

language_patterns.description: Settings for detecting AI-characteristic language choices: overused buzzwords, hedging phrases, filler expressions, and meta-commentary.

enabled.description: Master toggle for all language pattern detection. When false, all language pattern checks are skipped.

buzzwords.description: Detect AI-favored buzzwords and cliches such as 'delve', 'leverage', 'tapestry', 'landscape', 'robust', 'comprehensive'.

hedging.description: Detect excessive hedging language ('it's worth noting', 'it's important to remember', 'arguably') that AI uses to appear balanced.

filler.description: Detect filler phrases ('in today's world', 'at the end of the day', 'when it comes to') that pad AI-generated text.

meta_commentary.description: Detect self-referential meta-commentary ('as an AI', 'let me explain', 'in this article we will explore') characteristic of AI output.

structural_patterns.description: Settings for detecting AI-characteristic document structures: excessive lists, formulaic groupings, and parallel constructions.

enabled.description: Master toggle for all structural pattern detection. When false, all structural pattern checks are skipped.

list_addiction.description: Detect over-reliance on bulleted and numbered lists where prose would be more natural and appropriate.

rule_of_three.description: Detect the AI tendency to group ideas in exactly three items (three bullet points, three examples, three adjectives).

from_x_to_y.description: Detect the 'from X to Y' parallel construction pattern ('from planning to execution', 'from novice to expert') favored by AI.

voice_patterns.description: Settings for detecting AI-characteristic voice and style markers: passive constructions, generic analogies, and unnaturally perfect grammar.

enabled.description: Master toggle for all voice pattern detection. When false, all voice pattern checks are skipped.

passive_voice.description: Detect excessive passive voice constructions that AI models default to for a formal, detached tone.

generic_analogies.description: Detect generic, overused analogies and metaphors ('like a well-oiled machine', 'the tip of the iceberg') common in AI writing.

perfect_grammar.description: Detect unnaturally flawless grammar and sentence construction that lacks the minor imperfections of authentic human writing.

fix.description: Settings that control the auto-fix behavior when the plugin corrects detected AI patterns in content files.

dry_run_by_default.description: When true, the fix command previews changes without modifying files unless --apply is explicitly passed.

backup_files.description: When true, create a .bak copy of each file before applying fixes. Useful as a safety net when dry_run_by_default is disabled.

report_format.description: Level of detail in the fix report. 'minimal' shows only counts, 'normal' shows each replacement, 'detailed' includes surrounding context.

output.description: Settings that control the format and verbosity of detection scan output.

verbosity.description: Output verbosity level. 'minimal' shows summary counts only, 'normal' lists each finding, 'detailed' adds context snippets and explanations.

format.description: Output format for scan results. 'markdown' produces human-readable reports, 'json' produces machine-parseable structured output.

show_line_numbers.description: Include line numbers in scan output to help locate findings in source files.

max_results_per_tier.description: Maximum number of findings to report per severity tier. Prevents output overload on files with many detections.

interview.description: Voice elicitation interview settings. Controls session parameters, quality gates, scoring algorithms, deep-dive limits, and profile output paths.

session_storage.description: Directory path where interview session state files are persisted. Defaults to $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/sessions when running as a plugin, or ~/.human-voice/sessions in standalone mode.

total_estimated_minutes.description: Estimated total duration of the interview in minutes. Used for pacing questions and progress indicators.

estimated_questions.description: Target number of questions for a complete interview session. The engine adapts based on respondent engagement and branch depth.

format_streak_limit.description: Maximum consecutive questions of the same format (e.g., Likert scale) before the engine forces a format change to maintain engagement.

default_branch.description: The initial writing domain branch used to seed the interview. Determines the starting question set and vocabulary calibration context.

quality.description: Response quality monitoring thresholds. These gates detect low-effort or inattentive responses and trigger engagement resets or session invalidation.

straightlining_threshold.description: Number of consecutive identical responses on scaled questions before flagging as straightlining (respondent selecting the same value repeatedly).

speed_threshold_ms.description: Minimum response time in milliseconds. Responses faster than this threshold on qualifying questions are flagged as speed-rushing.

speed_exempt_below_seconds.description: Questions with expected reading time below this many seconds are exempt from speed-rushing checks (e.g., simple binary choices).

speed_applies_above_seconds.description: Speed-rushing detection only applies to questions with expected reading time above this many seconds (longer, more complex prompts).

alternation_threshold.description: Number of consecutive alternating extreme-value responses (e.g., 1-7-1-7) before flagging as pattern alternation.

alternation_extreme_values.description: Scale endpoint values considered 'extreme' for alternation detection. Typically the min and max of the Likert scale.

engagement_reset_min_flags.description: Minimum number of quality flags required within a rolling window before triggering an engagement reset prompt.

engagement_reset_max_per_session.description: Maximum number of engagement resets allowed per session. Exceeding this limit contributes to session invalidation.

session_invalid_threshold.description: Total number of quality flags that triggers automatic session invalidation, discarding results as unreliable.

confidence_penalty_per_flag.description: Confidence score reduction applied for each quality flag. Accumulates across all flag types during the session.

attention_checks.description: Attention check question settings. Embedded trap questions verify the respondent is reading and engaging with each prompt.

min_checks_passed.description: Minimum number of attention checks that must be passed for the session to be considered valid.

confidence_penalty_per_failure.description: Confidence score reduction applied for each failed attention check. Larger than per-flag penalties because attention failures are more serious.

scoring.description: Voice dimension scoring algorithm parameters. Controls how self-report and observed measurements are weighted and how reliability is assessed.

dimension_sd_weight.description: Weight given to the semantic differential score within each dimension's composite calculation. The remainder goes to the normalized raw score.

sd_normalized_weight.description: Weight given to the normalized standard deviation component. Complements dimension_sd_weight; the two should sum to 1.0.

min_cronbach_alpha.description: Minimum acceptable Cronbach's alpha for internal consistency reliability. Dimensions scoring below this threshold are flagged as unreliable.

reliability_tiers.description: Grouping of voice dimensions into reliability assessment tiers. Tier 1 dimensions have more redundant items and are expected to achieve higher alpha values.

tier_1.description: Tier 1 voice dimensions with the highest item redundancy and expected reliability. These dimensions drive the core voice profile.

tier_2.description: Tier 2 voice dimensions with fewer redundant items. These dimensions supplement the profile but may have wider confidence intervals.

elicitation.description: Adaptive elicitation settings. Controls when and how the interviewer probes thin or vague responses to extract richer voice data. Probes are follow-up questions asked by the agent before advancing to the next sequencer question.

enabled.description: Master toggle for adaptive elicitation. When false, the interviewer never probes — it accepts every response as-is and advances.

max_probes_per_question.description: Maximum follow-up probes the interviewer may ask for a single question before accepting the response and moving on.

eligible_types.description: Question types eligible for adaptive probing. Scale-based types (likert, forced_choice, semantic_differential, select) are never probed since their responses are structurally complete.

min_words_open_ended.description: Minimum word count for an open_ended response to be considered adequate. Responses below this threshold are candidates for probing.

min_words_writing_sample.description: Minimum word count for a writing_sample response. The question's target word count takes precedence when specified; this is the fallback floor.

min_words_scenario.description: Minimum word count for scenario and projective responses to be considered adequate.

vagueness_indicators.description: Phrases that signal a vague or non-committal response. When detected in eligible question types, the interviewer probes for specifics. Case-insensitive matching.

deep_dives.description: Deep-dive follow-up settings. Deep dives are extended question sequences that probe a specific dimension in greater detail when initial responses suggest complexity.

max_per_session.description: Maximum number of deep-dive sequences allowed per interview session. Set to 0 to disable deep dives entirely.

time_budget_minutes.description: Time budget in minutes allocated per deep-dive sequence. The engine uses this to limit follow-up question count.

profile.description: Profile generation and output settings. Controls where the final voice profile and prompt injection artifacts are written.

publish_to.description: File path where the completed voice profile JSON is written after a successful interview. Supports tilde expansion.

injection_to.description: File path where the generated voice prompt injection text is written. This file is designed to be included in AI system prompts to guide writing style.

population_means.description: Population baseline statistics (mean and standard deviation) for each voice dimension, used to compute distinctive-feature z-scores and identify where a writer diverges from the norm.

additionalProperties.description: Baseline statistics for a single voice dimension.

mean.description: Population mean score for this voice dimension on the measurement scale.

sd.description: Population standard deviation for this voice dimension. Must be non-negative.
